title: The Top 20 Energy ETFs of the Past 3 Years
h1: Financial Planning: The Top 20 Energy ETFs of the Past 3 Years
metaDescription: The top 20 energy ETFs highlight oil, solar, wind, and natural gas performance trends. See which energy ETFs delivered the strongest returns.
excerpt: Global energy markets have delivered notable returns over the past three years, despite facing significant geopolitical upheaval and economic uncertainty. The top-performing energy ETFs have provided investors with oppor

blocks.text: Global energy markets have delivered notable returns over the past three years, despite facing significant geopolitical upheaval and economic uncertainty. The top-performing energy ETFs have provided investors with opportunities to gain exposure to both traditional oil and gas companies as well as alternative energy sectors like solar and wind. While some funds experienced short-term declines amid recent market volatility, the broader trend has highlighted the sector’s ability to act as a strategic complement within diversified investment portfolios.
Analysts note that energy ETFs can help capture sector-specific growth while reducing individual stock risk, making them valuable tools for long-term investment strategies.

Within this landscape, traditional energy ETFs such as those tracking large integrated oil companies and oilfield services remain key components for balanced portfolios. At the same time, policy support for renewable energy, decarbonization trends, and expanding infrastructure have positioned solar and wind ETFs for growth, although returns may fluctuate in response to political and regulatory changes.
Though experts caution against over-concentration in any single sector given ongoing price and supply uncertainties, energy ETFs offer investors both income and potential inflation hedging.

Dean Lyulkin , co-CEO of Cardiff, recommends a measured approach to energy investing, stating that energy should remain at market weight within broader portfolios rather than taking aggressive, sector-specific positions, pairing broad-based energy funds with targeted oilfield services ETFs to achieve diversified exposure without making outsized directional bets on oil prices.
By focusing on market-weight allocations and blending traditional and renewable energy investments, investors can manage risk while remaining positioned to benefit from sector growth.
